tagline: Clean-room implementation of 90's era ANSI BBSes
business_goal: BBSes were apart of a lot of hackers childhoods and this is a fun project to pay homage to that.
users: People with too much time on their hands.
current_process: There is no manual process. This is a greenfield implementation.
terminology: Message Board - Place for people to leave messages with each other.
Door Games - Dumb Text Games that can be launched from inside the BBS
File Library - Library of objects users can download to their computers
business_rules: A BBS is a system used to connect people across the internet with common interests.
decisions: The BBS must have ansi graphics when availble to the user, but must support older terminal types in black and white
The bbs must be security hardened against buffer overflows, packet ejection and sandbox escape tactics.
The BBS must have a user message board, a file library, door games, and private messaging features.
risks: mythos level AI systems that can login to the BBS and and escape into the parent OS
fragile_inputs: With terminal emulation there were a variety of attack surfaces that hackers exploited. We need to harden against all known and unknown attacks of this nature.
open_questions: How long per day each user should be allowed to login (in minutes)
How to work the manual approval process for membership applications.
All the features and utilities that BBSes have offered in the past
the legal aspects of letting others inside my information systems.
sprint1_goal: Figure out what common architectures BBSes have been written in. LOok for opensource BBS implemntations and review their tech stacks. Make Design Decisions for the product
sprint1_acceptance: SprintPlanning.md will have a plan to implement each part of the BBS
state_current: Sprint 001 (Discovery & Architecture) — interview complete, planning files populated. Awaiting Architect review.
state_next: Review planning/ files with the Architect; resolve QUESTIONS.md; produce Sprint 002 requirements.
